e_visa.nationality: All nationalities not exempt from visa
e_visa.notes: All visa applications processed online via the official eVisa portal at https://evisa.immigration.gov.ag/agEvisa-app/. The system allows applicants to apply online; an approval or denial letter is sent once processed. Processing time approximately 5 working days. A soft or hard copy of the eVisa should be carried. Immigration officers at ports of entry reserve the right to deny entry even to eVisa holders.

visa_policy.notes: Official immigration.gov.ag pages scraped successfully via Firecrawl (June 2026). The complete enumerated country-by-country list from immigration.gov.ag/visa-services/countries-exempt-from-visa/ is now reflected per-nationality in visa_free. Cuba appears ONLY in the footnote text of the official page and is NOT listed as a table entry — its visa-free status for 30 days is referenced in the footnote ('Citizens of China and Cuba are exempt from the visa requirements if their travel do not exceed thirty (30) days') but Cuba does not appear in any of the three exemption tables; Cuba has been noted as a gap. Citizens not on the exempt list require a visa (eVisa). Cruise ship visitors arriving morning/departing same evening and same-day transit passengers staying within controlled airport space are not mentioned on the fetched pages.

conditional_access.conditions: Single entry tourist visa-on-arrival. Fee US$100 per passenger (including children) or equivalent Eastern Caribbean Currency. Non-extendable and non-convertible except for medical emergencies. Not applicable to diplomatic or official passport holders.

cbi.program_name: Antigua and Barbuda Citizenship by Investment Programme

options.notes: Non-refundable contribution to the National Development Fund (NDF). Minimum US$230,000 per application. Processing fees: US$10,000 (single applicant), US$20,000 (family up to 4), US$20,000 + US$10,000 per additional dependent from 5th onwards. Due diligence fee: US$8,500 (principal), US$5,000 (spouse), free (child 0-11), US$2,000 (child 12-17), US$4,000 (dependent adult 18+ or parent 55+). Passport fee: US$300 per person.

Purchase of approved real estate project with minimum value US$300,000. Property cannot be re-sold until 5 years after purchase unless an alternative approved real estate is purchased. Processing fees same structure as NDF option. Same due diligence and passport fee structure.

Investment in an approved business. Single applicant minimum: US$1,500,000. Joint investment (minimum 2 persons): total US$5,000,000 with each person contributing at least US$400,000. Businesses must receive prior approval from the CIU. min_amount reflects per-person minimum for joint investment.

Contribution to the University of the West Indies (UWI) Fund. Minimum US$260,000 for a family of six or more (processing fees included per official page). One family member receives a one-year tuition-only scholarship at UWI. Families of 7 or more incur an additional US$10,000 per additional dependent. Families of fewer than 6 are not eligible for this option.

cbi.processing_time: Not stated verbatim on cip.gov.ag fetched pages
cbi.notes: Program established 2013. August 2024 price increase raised NDF minimum from US$100,000 to US$230,000. October 2025 reform: mandatory physical residency increased from 5 days to 30 days within the first 5 years of obtaining citizenship (multiple short visits count toward the 30-day total). Mandatory interview (in-person or remote) introduced for all applicants. Restricted Countries List (Afghanistan, Iran, North Korea, Somalia, Yemen, Sudan) confirmed on cip.gov.ag/faq/ — nationals may apply only if they migrated before age of majority OR maintained permanent residence outside those countries for at least 10 years. Applicants must take an oath of allegiance either in Antigua and Barbuda or at a diplomatic mission abroad. All fees subject to change per official schedule page.

programs.name: Antigua and Barbuda Permanent Residence Scheme
programs.notes: Introduced in 1995. Targets high-net-worth individuals. Requirements: demonstrated minimum annual income of USD $100,000; payment of flat annual tax of USD $20,000; minimum physical presence of 30 days per year in Antigua and Barbuda. PDF could not be directly fetched; document is referenced from immigration.gov.ag/general-services/residency/.

programs.name: Standard Resident Permit (Class A)
programs.notes: Available to any person who has been legally and ordinarily resident in Antigua and Barbuda for at least 4 years, or married to an Antiguan citizen for at least 1 year. Standard immigration residency route, not an investment program.

programs.name: Nomad Digital Residence Programme
programs.eligibility: Programme closed to new applications as of November 15, 2025. Previously required remote work capability and demonstrated minimum annual income of US$50,000 for each year of the 2-year stay. Valid medical insurance and police clearance were required.
programs.notes: The Antigua and Barbuda NDR Programme was officially discontinued effective November 15, 2025 per an official government announcement (ab.gov.ag). Previously: fees were US$1,500 (single applicant), US$2,000 (couple), US$3,000 (family of 3 or more); granted special-resident authorisation valid for up to 2 years; included income tax exemption on foreign-sourced income.

agreements: CARICOM (Caribbean Community) — free movement of skilled nationals under CSME

OECS (Organisation of Eastern Caribbean States) — free movement under Revised Treaty of Basseterre / OECS Economic Union; indefinite stay for OECS Protocol State nationals (Dominica, Grenada, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines)
Commonwealth of Nations — preferential entry treatment among member states

validation_notes: CHANGED: visa_free expanded from 6 broad-group entries to per-nationality entries covering all 100+ countries enumerated on the official immigration.gov.ag page (scraped directly via Firecrawl June 2026). Previous record used groupings ('Most EU citizens', 'CARICOM member state nationals', 'Commonwealth countries nationals') which were replaced by individual-country entries matching the official tables exactly.

CHANGED: Cuba removed from visa_free table entries. The official page footnote says 'Citizens of China and Cuba are exempt from the visa requirements if their travel do not exceed thirty (30) days' but Cuba does NOT appear in any of the three country tables on the page. Cuba's status is noted as a gap pending future official confirmation.

CHANGED: USA, Japan, South Korea, UAE, Russia, Switzerland, Norway, Brazil, Mexico, Argentina, Turkey, and many other countries added to visa_free — they appeared in the 'Other Countries' table on immigration.gov.ag which was not previously retrieved.
CHANGED: Serbia added to visa_free (appears in the EU table on the official page though Serbia is not an EU member; classified as-listed with explanatory note).

CHANGED: conditional_access UK credential subtype updated from 'ILR / settlement (Indefinite Leave to Remain or UK Resident Card)' to 'UK Resident Card (residence permit)' matching exact wording on official VoA page.
CHANGED: fast_track Nomad Digital Residence Programme marked as DISCONTINUED effective 2025-11-15 per official government announcement (ab.gov.ag); was previously listed as active.
CHANGED: CBI residency requirement updated from '5 days within first 5 years' to '30 days within first 5 years' reflecting October 2025 parliamentary reform.
CHANGED: CBI notes updated to reflect mandatory interview requirement introduced in October 2025 reform.
CHANGED: CBI processing_time set to 'Not stated verbatim on cip.gov.ag fetched pages' (removed the previously unverified 3-6 month claim).

CONFIRMED: CBI investment minimums (NDF US$230,000, Real Estate US$300,000, Business US$1,500,000 solo / US$5,000,000 joint / US$400,000 per person, UWI US$260,000) verified on cip.gov.ag investment option pages.
CONFIRMED: CBI due diligence fees (US$8,500 principal, US$5,000 spouse, free child 0-11, US$2,000 child 12-17, US$4,000 adult 18+ / parent 55+) verified on cip.gov.ag/schedule-of-fees/.
CONFIRMED: VoA credential list (US visa/Green Card, Canadian visa/PR, UK visa/Resident Card, Schengen visa/Resident Card) verified directly on immigration.gov.ag/visa-services/visa-on-arrival/.
CONFIRMED: Restricted Countries List (Afghanistan, Iran, North Korea, Somalia, Yemen, Sudan) verified on cip.gov.ag/faq/.
gaps: Cuba: The official page footnote cites Cuba for the 30-day exemption alongside China, but Cuba does not appear in any of the three enumption tables on immigration.gov.ag/visa-services/countries-exempt-from-visa/. Cuba's visa-free status could not be confirmed from the table; excluded from visa_free pending clarification.
legalaffairs.gov.ag Permanent Residence Scheme PDF could not be fetched (SSL/404 errors); RBI details for the tax residency scheme are from previous validated record.
CBI processing time (3-6 months) is widely cited in secondary sources but not found verbatim on cip.gov.ag pages fetched.

UAE visa waiver agreement (diplomatic/official passports) mentioned in earlier research — terms and whether it also covers ordinary passports not confirmed from the current official immigration.gov.ag page fetch.
The official page lists 'United Kingdom (overseas Territories) Vanuatu' in a single cell; exact scope of UK overseas territories included is unclear from the page formatting.
nomad.gov.ag main portal returned SSL error; NDR discontinuation confirmed from search snippets citing an official ab.gov.ag PDF announcement, but the PDF itself returned 404 at time of check.

October 2025 CBI reforms (30-day residency requirement, mandatory interviews) confirmed from multiple secondary sources citing parliamentary legislation but not yet reflected on cip.gov.ag FAQ or main pages fetched.

visa_free.name: British Overseas Territories (Akrotiri and Dhekelia, Anguilla, Bermuda, British Virgin Islands, Cayman Islands, Gibraltar, Guernsey, Jersey, Montserrat, Pitcairn Islands, St. Helena, Turks and Caicos)

French Overseas Departments and Collectives (French Guiana, French Polynesia, French Southern and Antarctic Lands, Guadeloupe, Martinique, New Caledonia, Saint Barthelemy, Saint Martin, Saint Pierre and Miquelon, Wallis and Futuna)
Dutch Territories (Aruba, Bonaire, Curacao, Saba, St. Eustatius, St. Maarten)

outbound_official.notes: Source is cip.gov.ag (Citizenship by Investment Programme), an official .gov.ag domain. The page section 'Reciprocal Visa-free Access for Diplomatic, Official and/or Ordinary Passport holders of Antigua and Barbuda' lists countries where ATG ordinary passport holders are admitted without a prior consular visa. Countries marked * or ++ are visa-on-arrival (classified under visa_on_arrival). Countries marked ** (Argentina, United Arab Emirates) are reciprocal only for Diplomatic and Official passports — NOT ordinary passports — and are excluded from both lists here. The foreignaffairs.gov.ag domain (Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Trade and Barbuda Affairs) exists but had SSL certificate errors preventing fetch at time of research (June 2026); no dedicated outbound visa-free page was found on that domain or any other official ATG government domain. No date stamp appears on the cip.gov.ag page.
